search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG208 heart murmur echocardiography recommendations
site:escardio.org valvular heart disease murmur guideline
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
The NICE source is adult-specific and paediatric pathways were not substituted.
Procedure and treatment content was not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults with suspected or established valve disease or a murmur; the legacy workflow has no adult-only restriction.
setting_applicability: Direct for primary, secondary, and specialist adult murmur assessment and monitoring.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local echocardiography and referral pathways.
recency_verification: The current official NICE NG208 recommendations were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG208 remains the current official NICE guideline for adult heart valve disease.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped murmur identity, symptom, family-history, murmur-character, peripheral-sign, ECG, echocardiography, preset, and structured-history fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports murmur nature, family and medical history, symptoms, peripheral signs, abnormal ECG, and clinician-led echocardiography review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped exertional, syncope, referral, and echocardiography follow-up fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports exertional syncope, severe angina or breathlessness, urgent specialist assessment, echocardiography, and referral after echocardiography.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-arranged follow-up and repeat echocardiographic monitoring fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged review and repeat echocardiographic monitoring for established valve disease.

unresolved_source_gaps: Children and paediatric murmurs are outside the exact source.
Generic vital-sign fields, murmur grade, generic clinician plans, and generic safety-netting remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, valve interpretation, referral urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
